ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-16-2009 Continued To Dec-21-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Royce for Patricia Ellis.

Mr. Royce stated that Mr. Sherman was ill, that there was a waiver of accounting and that the verification was signed by Mr. Young but that Ms. Ellis was away on a trip. In addition, he stated that the bond was never filed, that the bond was waived by both Parties, that the real property was sold and that the only thing left to do was to distribute assets. The matter was continued to December 21, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Off Calendar Sep-09-2009 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Colleen Coen and Gary Royce for Patricia Ellis. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator.

The Court stated that it was prepared to sign the stipulation. Ms. Coen stated that the other petitions could be dropped. The Court signed the Stipulation and Order For Settlement of Objections, etc. and all three petitions before the Court were taken off calendar. Status on filing of the Petition For Final Distribution was set for November 4, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Petition For Issuance Of Order Compelling Administrator Patricia Ellis To Discolse Her Activities On Behalf Of The Estate; Declarations; Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-27-2009 Continued To Sep-09-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Royce for Patricia Ellis.

Mr. Royce stated that Ms. Ellis filed a bond, that the letters had not been issued and that Mr. Young the Co-Administrator had not procured a bond. In addition, he stated that mediation had been completed and that they had resolved three issues raised in the objections that were filed. Furthermore, Mr. Royce stated that Mr. Young agreed to pay his Client $15,000.00 to settle all disputes, that a stipulation and order had been prepared, that it was being reviewed by Mr. Young, that the property needed to be marketed and that the Parties were working together. The matter was continued to September 9, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further status.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Janet Pond, CSR#5292. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

Off Calendar Jan-26-2009 Advanced From Mar-18-09 To Feb-10-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Issuance Of Order Compelling Administrator Patricia Ellis To Discolse Her Activities On Behalf Of The Estate; Declarations; Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Mar-18-2009 Continued To May-05-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator ("SFPA"). Gary Royce for Patricia Ellis. Richard Sherman appeared by telephone for Robert Young.

Mr. Royce conferred with Mr. Sherman this morning regarding the appointment of a Mediator or a private Judge. The Parties suggested continuing the matter for three weeks to determine whether the Judge would have the authority to make decisions on the matter. Mr. Ferdon expressed concern about the real property and stated that he needed instruction as to the disposition of the property. The Court relieved the SFPA as the Administrator of the estate and re-appointed Patricia Ellis and Robert Young as Co-Administrators. Bond in the amount of $150,000.00 is required. The matters were continued to May 5, 2009 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on mediation and for the setting of the other matters. Mr. Sherman will appear by Court call.

Off Calendar Feb-10-2009 Continued To Feb-25-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Of Former Personal Representative, Patricia Ellis, For Approval Of First Account And Request For Reservation Of Issue Of Compensation Of Personal Representative
Off Calendar Feb-10-2009 Continued To Feb-25-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set-Aside Order
Off Calendar Feb-10-2009 Continued To Feb-25-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Issuance Of Order Compelling Administrator Patricia Ellis To Discolse Her Activities On Behalf Of The Estate; Declarations; Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jan-06-2009 Continued To Feb-10-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Gary Royce for Patricia Ellis (Former Successor Administratix). Richard Sherman for and with Robert Young appeared by telephone.

Mr. Royce stated that Ms. Ellis extended the listing agreement with Coldwell Banker and that there were no bids on the property as the listing price was not attractive to potential buyers. In addition, he asked the Court to reconsider re-appointing Ms. Ellis as Administratix based on the agreement with Mr. Sherman and stated that he would like a hearing on the accounting regarding expenses. Mr. Sherman stated that he had not received any objections to Mr. Young's accounting and suggested all parties proceed with their accountings to allow time to file objections if any. Mr. Ferdon expressed concern with the Public Administrator having liability over the property, stated that he had no proof of insurance, no proof of taxes being paid, no proof of payments for any obligations and requested Mr. Royce and Mr. Sherman provide all documents with regard to these matters and all assets. In addition, Mr. Ferdon stated that he had no information regarding Coldwell Banker's involvement with the listing/showing of the property and requested this information be provided to him as well. The Court directed all Parties to turn over all said documents to Mr. Ferdon. The matter was continued to February 10, 2009. The Court ordered Patricia Ellis' accounting and Robert Young's accounting be filed no later than January 21, 2009. Objections are due by February 2, 2009 and the petitions are to be heard on February 10, 2009. The Motion To Set Aside Order on calendar for March 16, 2009 was advanced to February 10, 2009.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Shannon.

Petition For Issuance Of Order Compelling Administrator Patricia Ellis To Discolse Her Activities On Behalf Of The Estate; Declarations; Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Dec-10-2008 Continued To Jan-06-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Issuance Of Order Compelling Administrator Patricia Ellis To Discolse Her Activities On Behalf Of The Estate; Declarations; Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Oct-29-2008 Continued To Dec-10-08 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Sherman appeared by telephone for Robert Young. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA).

Mr. Sherman stated that Mr. Royce conceded to all points he raised and that they were willing to let Ms. Ellis continue to serve. The Court informed Mr. Sherman that Ms. Ellis would be removed and that the Public Administrator would be appointed. Mr. Ferdon accepted the appointment and stated that an accounting would be needed by both parties unless they waived the accountings and that the SFPA would proceed with the sale of the property. Mr. Sherman raised concern with the current housing market, requested the property not be sold at this time and stated that there were sufficient funds to pay for all obligations. Mr. Ferdon will not put the property on the market until he receives further instructions from both parties. All prior Administrators are to do an accounting. The matter was continued to December 10, 2008 at 10:30 a.m. for further status. Mr. Sherman will appear by Court Call.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda/R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Brown.

Petition For Issuance Of Order Compelling Administrator Patricia Ellis To Discolse Her Activities On Behalf Of The Estate; Declarations; Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Oct-15-2008 Continued To Oct-29-08 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Issuance Of Order Compelling Administrator Patricia Ellis To Discolse Her Activities On Behalf Of The Estate; Declarations; Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Oct-01-2008 Continued To Oct-15-08 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Sherman appeared by telephone for Robert Young. Sean Joyce specially appeared for Gary Royce for Patricia Ellis.

Mr. Sherman stated that he did not receive a response from Mr. Royce, that he did not receive copies of the recently filed documents, and that Ms. Ellis was unwilling to communicate. In addition, he stated that they were not being informed as to the status of the property, and Mr. Royce and Ms. Ellis were in violation of the prior agreement. Mr. Joyce stated that the copies mailed to Mr. Sherman had been returned as undeliverable, that the phone number he had on record for Mr. Sherman was disconnected, and that the property was listed through MSL. The Court stated that the matter was not moving forward, that the daughter failed to inventory all of the assets, and that the Court would appoint the Public Administrator to take over. Mr. Sherman provided Mr. Joyce with his phone number and fax number. Mr. Joyce stated that he would fax copies of the documents and provide all information to Mr. Sherman. The matter was continued to October 15, 2008 at 10:30 a.m for status on progress of the Final Distribution. A court call appearance for Mr. Sherman was approved.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

Petition For Issuance Of Order Compelling Administrator Patricia Ellis To Discolse Her Activities On Behalf Of The Estate; Declarations; Points And Authorities

Petition For Removal Of Robert Young As Representative, Surrender Of Property And For Damages For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty
Off Calendar Dec-07-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Hanson in Pro Per. Gary Royce for Patricia Ellis. Rich Sherman (appeared by phone) for Robert Young (present in court).

Ms. Hanson objected to Mr. Sherman appearing by phone stating that she was notified 5 days in advance. In addition, she expressed that the OSC was inappropriate, that the papers were procedurally inaccurate, and that it was a retaliation against her for removal of the petition. Mr. Sherman stated that the OSC was based upon false statements made by Ms. Hanson. Mr. Royce submitted the order regarding settlement and the original settlement agreement. Mr. Sherman did not receive the fax copy of the order. Article 2 was amended to read Letters will be issued with limited authority. Order for appointment of single administrator and surrender of possession of estate property was submitted.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Rich Lee.

Petition For Issuance Of Order To Show Cause Why Elizabeth Hansen Should Not Be Held In Contempt Of Court
Off Calendar Oct-29-2007 Continued To Dec-04-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of Robert Young As Representative, Surrender Of Property And For Damages For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty
Off Calendar Oct-29-2007 Continued To Dec-04-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Royce for Patricia Ellis. Elizabeth Hansen (former counsel for Patricia Ellis and respondent to OSC). Richard Sherman appeared by phone for Robert Young (also present).

Mr. Royce informed the Court that the Substitution of Attorney was executed this morning and will be filed today. The Court suggested referral of both matters to Pro Bono Mediation. Mr. Royce stated that he did not see any room for mediation, that Mr. Young intended to remain in the property and requested the immediate removal of Mr. Young as the Co-Administrator. Ms. Hansen stated that the petition was vexatious and she did not see how anything could be mediated. Mr. Sherman expressed that mediatiion would be appropriate for the removal of Mr. Young. Ms. Hansen objected to Mr. Sherman's appearance by phone without proper notice. The Court referred the Petition for Removal of Mr. Young to Pro Bono Mediation signing the Order for the same. Mediation is to be completed by November 30, 2007. The Status Conference is set for December 04, 2007.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Reporter: Maureen Howard CSR#7156. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: T. Bonner.

Petition For Removal Of Robert Young As Representative, Surrender Of Property And For Damages For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty
Off Calendar Sep-12-2007 Advanced From Oct-22-07 To Oct-15-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of Robert Young As Representative, Surrender Of Property And For Damages For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty
Off Calendar Oct-15-2007 Continued To Oct-29-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Elizabeth Hanson appeared for and with Patricia Ellis (Petitioner for Removal). Ms. Hanson also appeared as Respondent to the Contempt Petition. Richard Sherman appeared for and with Robert Young (Petitioner for the Contempt Petition).

Ms. Hanson requested a two week continuance for her client to obtain new counsel. Mr. Sherman objected to the continuance stating that he was not contacted regarding the continuance. The Court is concerned that an Inventory and Appraisal has not been filed by the Probate Referee and that the Co-Administrators can not get along. The Court stated that Ms. Hanson should be given time to ensure that her client could seek new counsel. The Court continued both matters to October 29, 2007 for status.

Judge: Herbert Donaldson. Reporter: Patrick Heard CSR#11055. Bailiff: A. Aguerre. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.
